# AgentCollect

> AI-powered debt collection that replaces collection agencies. Trusted by Microsoft, Dell, Plaid, Checkr, and more.

## What AgentCollect Does

AgentCollect replaces traditional collection agencies with AI agents and licensed attorneys. Businesses upload a spreadsheet of unpaid invoices. AI agents research decision-makers, then contact debtors via phone calls, emails, and SMS — under the client's brand. If needed, a partnered licensed attorney sends formal demand letters with legal weight. The debtor pays the creditor directly. No middleman.

## Key Facts

- Founded: 2020 (Y Combinator S23)
- Founder: John Banner
- Headquarters: San Francisco, CA
- Website: https://www.agentcollect.com
- Contact: j@agentcollect.com

## How It Works

1. Upload: Client uploads a CSV or Excel file with unpaid invoices. No API integration required. Live in 20 minutes.
2. AI Collects: AI agents find decision-makers, then contact debtors via voice, email, and SMS under the client's brand.
3. Attorney Escalation: If AI outreach is insufficient, a licensed attorney sends formal demand letters on behalf of the client.
4. Direct Payment: Debtors pay the client directly through a branded payment portal. Not through an agency.

## Products

### AI Agent Collection
- White-label AI agents call, email, and negotiate under the client's brand name
- Multi-channel: voice calls, email, SMS, WhatsApp
- 24/7 operation
- Contact enrichment and decision-maker research included
- Results in approximately 20 days

### Attorney Collection
- A licensed attorney officially represents the client
- Formal demand letters with bar number and legal address
- Legal weight that collection agencies cannot provide
- Branded payment portal under the law firm's name
- Plug and play: just upload an Excel file

## Pricing

- Success-only fees: clients pay nothing unless AgentCollect recovers money
- No setup fees, no minimums, no long-term contracts
- No license fee for clients with $1M+ monthly receivables volume
- $0 upfront

## Clients

Microsoft, Dell Technologies, Plaid, Checkr, DoorDash, Twilio, Redis, Uber Freight

## Differentiators vs Collection Agencies

| AgentCollect | Collection Agency |
|---|---|
| Success-only fees | 25-35% commission |
| Results in ~20 days | 4-6 months typical |
| 1 AI agent per account | 1 human per 250 accounts |
| Up to 85,000 recoveries per day | Limited by headcount |
| 12-month mandate | Give up after 90 days |
| Attorney mode: 70% email open rate | 20% email open rate |
| Contact Finder: +130% contacts enriched | Manual skip tracing |
| 90% of disputes resolved instantly by AI | Weeks of back-and-forth |
| Debtor pays client directly | Debtor pays the agency |
| Licensed attorney, bar number | No legal standing |
| Zero compliance incidents | FDCPA violation risk |
| Client's brand on all communications | Agency's brand |
| Real-time dashboard | Quarterly reports |
| AI + attorney pipeline | Human call center |

## Compliance

- B2B debt collection only (business-to-business invoices, not consumer debt)
- SOC 2 Type II
- State-by-state compliance built into AI agents
- Zero compliance incidents across all clients
